message:
feat(doctor): add ephemeral beads health checks

Add gt doctor checks for ephemeral beads repos:
- ephemeral-exists: Verify .beads-ephemeral/ exists for each rig (fixable)
- ephemeral-git: Verify it's a valid git repo (fixable)
- ephemeral-orphans: Detect molecules >24h old (needs manual review)
- ephemeral-size: Warn if repo >100MB
- ephemeral-stale: Detect molecules with no activity in last hour

Implements gt-3x0z.3.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-3x0z.10","title":"Phase 4.2: Witness/Refinery patrol molecules","description":"Extend ephemeral patrol pattern to Witness and Refinery.

## Witness Patrol Cycle

mol-witness-patrol-cycle:
1. scan-polecats: Check each polecat status
2. detect-stalls: Find stuck/idle polecats
3. nudge-or-escalate: Take action on stalls
4. log-status: Record cycle results
→ Squash with summary

## Refinery Patrol Cycle

mol-refinery-patrol-cycle:
1. scan-queue: Check merge queue
2. process-ready: Merge ready MRs
3. handle-failures: Deal with conflicts/failures
4. log-status: Record cycle results
→ Squash with summary

## Quiescent Mode

When no work: just log 'no activity' summary.
Witness and Refinery can sleep between cycles until triggered.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.103951-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.103951-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.10","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.104305-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.10","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.742967-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-3x0z.12","title":"Phase 5.2: Ephemeral error handling and recovery","description":"Handle edge cases in ephemeral molecule lifecycle.

## Scenarios

### Agent dies mid-molecule
- Ephemeral state persists
- Witness detects stall (no heartbeat)
- Options:
  a. Nudge agent to resume
  b. Reassign to new agent
  c. Escalate to Mayor

### Squash fails
- Retry with exponential backoff
- If persistent failure, escalate
- Don't lose the ephemeral state until squash succeeds

### Orphaned molecules
- Molecule started, never completed
- gt doctor detects (>24h old with no activity)
- Options:
  a. Manual review
  b. Auto-abandon with 'abandoned' digest
  c. Reassign

### Ephemeral repo corruption
- Re-init from scratch
- Active molecules are lost
- Main beads is source of truth for assigned work

## Implementation

Add error handling to:
- bd mol bond (handle init failures)
- bd squash (retry logic)
- gt doctor (recovery suggestions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:30.158784-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:30.158784-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.12","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:30.15923-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.12","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.886432-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-3x0z.4","title":"Phase 2.1: gt spawn --molecule bonds in ephemeral","description":"Make gt spawn molecule-aware with ephemeral bonding.

## New Flag

```bash
gt spawn --issue gt-xxx --molecule mol-polecat-work
```

## Behavior

1. Create polecat with fresh worktree (existing)
2. Bond molecule in ephemeral: `bd mol bond mol-polecat-work --ephemeral`
3. Link molecule root to source issue
4. Include molecule context in work assignment mail
5. Start session

## Work Assignment Mail

```
Subject: Work Assignment: Fix lifecycle bug [MOLECULE]

You are working on gt-rixa as part of molecule mol-polecat-work.

Molecule instance: eph-abc123
Current step: read-assignment (1/8)

Follow the molecule workflow. When complete, generate summary and squash.
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.082722-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.082722-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.084965-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.385365-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.457259-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Detection

1. Check for active ephemeral molecule assigned to current identity
2. Parse molecule progress (current step, total steps)
3. Show in prime output

🔧 Polecat furiosa, checking in.

📦 Molecule: mol-polecat-work (eph-abc123)

Run 'bd mol status' for full molecule state.

 {"id":"gt-3x0z.6","title":"Phase 2.3: Polecat CLAUDE.md molecule workflow protocol","description":"Update polecat prompting for molecule-based work.

## CLAUDE.md Updates

Add section on molecule workflow:

```markdown
## Molecule Workflow

When assigned a molecule (check gt prime output):

1. Follow molecule steps in order
2. Mark steps complete: bd mol step complete <step-id>
3. Before signaling done:
   a. Generate summary of work performed
   b. Run: bd squash <molecule-root> --summary "<your summary>"
4. Then signal done as normal

### Summary Guidelines

Your summary should include:
- What was the task?
- What did you do?
- What was the outcome?
- Any issues or follow-ups?

Keep it to 2-4 sentences. This becomes the permanent record.

 {"id":"gt-bcwn","title":"Auto-handoff: self-cycling with guaranteed work pickup","description":"## Summary

A mechanism for agents to self-cycle: send handoff mail, request Deacon to kill the process, and automatically restart to pick up the work.

## Use Case

User says: `auto-handoff: gt-bug3`

The agent:
1. Sends handoff mail documenting current state
2. Requests Deacon to terminate the session
3. Deacon restarts the agent in background
4. New session picks up the handoff and continues working

When the user returns to terminal, the old session is gone but work continues in background. User can reattach.

## Key Requirements

- **Guaranteed pickup**: Something in the hook and/or handoff must ensure the new session picks up the work (not just a passive "check inbox")
- **Background execution**: Work continues without user presence
- **Reattachability**: User can reconnect to see progress

## Design Questions

1. Can molecules help coordinate this? (e.g., molecule binds the handoff + auto-restart)
2. What hook handles the pickup guarantee? 
3. How does Deacon know to restart vs just kill?
4. How does user reattach to a background agent?

## Related

- Molecules system
- Deacon lifecycle management
- Handoff hooks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:59:50.352286-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:00:06.867846-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-bfd","title":"Keepalive signal from bd/gt commands","description":"Every bd and gt command should touch a keepalive file to signal 'agent is alive/working'.

## Implementation

Touch `<workspace>/.gastown/keepalive.json`:
```json
{"last_command": "bd show gt-99m", "timestamp": "2025-12-18T13:45:00Z"}
```

## Usage by Daemon

- Fresh (< 2 min) → agent is working, skip heartbeat
- Stale (2-5 min) → might be thinking, gentle poke
- Very stale (> 5 min) → likely idle, safe to interrupt

## Benefits

- Zero cost (just file I/O)
- Works during long tool calls
- Doesn't require agent cooperation
- Foundation for smarter backoff strategies","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:03:43.816952-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:03:43.816952-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-bfd","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.407664-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Epic can specify merge_config in its description:
  merge_config:
    run_tests: true
    test_command: 'go test -race ./...'
    on_conflict: assign_back

When processing MRs for an epic:
1. Load rig-level merge_queue config
2. Check if epic has merge_config
3. Merge epic config over rig config
4. Use merged config for processing

Use cases:
- Risky refactors: more thorough testing
- Urgent fixes: skip some checks
- Experimental work: different test suite

 {"id":"gt-hcc0","title":"gt polecat remove --all: bulk polecat teardown","description":"Currently gt polecat remove only accepts one polecat at a time. Need bulk operations:

## Requested
- `gt polecat remove gastown --all` - remove all polecats from a rig
- `gt polecat remove gastown/A gastown/B ...` - remove multiple by name

## Context
After a swarm completes, tearing down 20 polecats one at a time is tedious.
Ephemeral workers should be easy to create and destroy in bulk.

## Related
- gt-c92: CLI: all command for batch polecat operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:06:43.892225-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:16:06.901839-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:16:06.901839-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-hgk","title":"Mail system: message types and threading","description":"GGT mail system needs message types and threading like PGT.

## 1. Message Types
Add to internal/mail/types.go:
```go
type MessageType string
const (
    TypeTask         MessageType = "task"         // Required processing
    TypeScavenge     MessageType = "scavenge"     // Optional first-come work
    TypeNotification MessageType = "notification" // Informational
    TypeReply        MessageType = "reply"        // Response to message
)

// Update Message struct
type Message struct {
    // existing fields...
    Type     MessageType `json:"type"`
    ThreadID string      `json:"thread_id,omitempty"`
    ReplyTo  string      `json:"reply_to,omitempty"`
}
```

## 2. Priority Levels
Expand from 2 to 4:
```go
type Priority string
const (
    PriorityLow    Priority = "low"
    PriorityNormal Priority = "normal"
    PriorityHigh   Priority = "high"
    PriorityUrgent Priority = "urgent"
)
```

## 3. CLI Updates
internal/cmd/mail.go:
- Add --type flag to send: `gt mail send ... --type task`
- Add --reply-to flag: `gt mail send ... --reply-to <msg-id>`
- Add thread command: `gt mail thread <thread-id>`

## 4. Threading Logic
NewMessage() should auto-generate thread_id if not a reply.
Reply messages inherit thread_id from original.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mail/types.go: Add types, expand Priority

 {"id":"gt-htto","title":"Heartbeat convention: simple liveness signal for agents","description":"Lightweight liveness signal extracted from Deacon epic (gt-5af).

**Implementation**: Each agent writes a timestamp file on activity:
```bash
echo '{"ts":"'$(date -Iseconds)'"}' > ~/gt/<role>/heartbeat.json
```

**Integration points**:
- SessionStart hook writes heartbeat
- Periodic activity (mail check, work completion) refreshes it
- `gt status` shows staleness (e.g., 'mayor: 5m ago')

**Weight**: ~5 lines per agent
**Value**: Quick debugging - see which agents are active at a glance

No monitoring daemon needed - human checks `gt status` when curious.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:40:45.459903-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:40:45.459903-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rana","title":"Patrol System: Agent lifecycle loops with attachments","description":"Enable Gas Town agents to run continuous patrols, survive crashes, and hand off work across sessions.

## Core Concepts
- **Attachments**: Molecules bound to agent's pinned bead until complete
- **Patrols**: Cyclic molecules that loop (deacon, witness, refinery)
- **Quiescent**: Agents that sleep until triggered (witness, refinery)

## Design Doc
See docs/patrol-system-design.md

## Phases
Phase 1: Foundation (attachment field, daemon detection, mol-deacon-patrol)
Phase 2: Quiescent Agents (wake triggers, witness/refinery patrols)
Phase 3: Callbacks and Plugins (mail protocol, plugin runner)
Phase 4: Polish (gt patrol status, metrics, tuning)

## Key Decisions
- Attachment as field on pinned bead (not edge type, for now)
- Mail-based orchestration for all callbacks
- Queue replacement for heartbeat delivery (not pile-up)
- Burn and respawn for patrol loops (not in-place reset)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00"}

- Ensure bd show displays attachment info

 {"id":"gt-rana.2","title":"Phase 1.2: Daemon attachment detection","description":"Daemon polls Deacon's pinned bead to detect naked state.

## Implementation
- Daemon knows Deacon's pinned bead ID
- Every heartbeat cycle: bd show <pinned> --json
- Parse attached_molecule field
- If null/empty: Deacon is naked

## Actions on Naked
- Spawn mol-deacon-patrol
- Attach to Deacon's pinned
- Nudge Deacon to start

## Failsafes
- Keepalive file monitoring
- Stale detection (>10min no progress)
- Force nudge on stale

 {"id":"gt-yx4","title":"Town root .beads has schema mismatch with bd","description":"The .beads directory at town root (/Users/stevey/gt/.beads) has an incompatible schema:

```
Error: failed to open database: failed to initialize schema: sqlite3: SQL logic error: no such column: thread_id
```

Meanwhile, gastown/.beads (symlinked to mayor/rig/.beads) works fine.

## Impact

- gt mail inbox fails at town root
- gt handoff sends mail to broken db
- Daemon can't check its inbox

## Options

1. Delete town root .beads/beads.db and let it recreate
2. Symlink town root .beads to gastown/.beads
3. Run schema migration on existing db

## Root Cause

Likely a beads version upgrade that added thread_id column, but the town root db was created before that.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:31:35.559042-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-z4g","title":"Plugin: Plan-to-Epic converter","description":"## Purpose

Help users create beads epics from various planning inputs.

## Inputs
- Markdown task lists
- GitHub issues
- Linear/Jira exports
- Free-form descriptions
- Existing beads epics

## Output
- Beads epic with properly structured children
- Dependencies set for wave ordering
- Priorities assigned
- Ready for `gt spawn --epic <id>`

## Implementation Options

### Option A: CLI Tool
```bash
gt plan import --from github --repo owner/repo --label batch-candidate
gt plan import --from markdown tasks.md
gt plan structure <epic-id>  # analyze and add dependencies
```

### Option B: Plugin Agent
A plugin at `<rig>/plugins/plan-oracle/` that:
- Receives planning requests via mail
- Analyzes scope and requirements
- Creates structured beads epic
- Sets dependencies based on analysis

### Option C: Interactive Mode
```bash
gt plan create
# Walks through questions, creates epic interactively
```

## Axiom

As stated: 'The Planning phase should end in the creation of a workable Beads plan.'

This plugin bridges the gap between human planning and machine-executable work.

## Priority

P2 - Nice to have for MVP. Manual epic creation works for now.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - output is just a beads epic with children. Workers process it independently.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T02:10:20.663549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:41.087304-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zivp","title":"mol-outpost-assign: Intelligent work routing to outposts","description":"The federation design shows outposts.yaml with a static policy section. Simple preference ordering is fine as config, but intelligent work routing should be a molecule.

Current static approach:
```yaml
policy:
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
```

Cases requiring cognition:
- "This is a long-running research task, route to VM not CloudRun"
- "This touches sensitive code, keep local"
- "These 5 issues are related, batch them to same outpost"
- "CloudRun cost is high today, prefer local even if slower"

## Molecule: outpost-assign
Intelligent work-to-outpost routing.

## Step: classify-work
Analyze the issue/work item:
- Expected duration (quick fix vs multi-hour)
- Resource requirements
- Sensitivity/security tier
- Related work (same epic?)

## Step: check-capacity
Query outpost status:
- Current load on each
- Cost accrued today
- Health status

## Step: select-outpost
Choose optimal outpost based on:
- Work classification
- Capacity/cost
- Policy constraints
Needs: classify-work, check-capacity

## Step: emit-assignment
Record decision in beads for audit.
Needs: select-outpost

## Notes
- This molecule is invoked by Mayor/Witness when spawning
- Simple cases can short-circuit to static policy
- Full analysis only for ambiguous cases","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00"}
